description: A configurable stand-in call provider. Produces a Result from its arguments alone, deterministically: a success carrying a chosen value (default: the dispatch's input) or a configured failure, after an optional delay, with optional window metadata.

value.description: The value of the success Result produced. Defaults to the dispatch's input. Unread when a non-null failure is supplied.

failure.description: A failure Result to produce instead of a success. Takes precedence over value when non-null; null is equivalent to absent: the success path.

type.description: The failure Result's type. MUST be a non-success type.

code.description: The failure code, the author's to choose; emulation is this provider's documented purpose.

previous.description: A chained prior failure. Defaults to absent; the mock emits the envelope exactly as configured.

delay.description: An ISO 8601 duration to wait before resolving.

metadata.description: Exposed verbatim as the window's provider.metadata.
The mock's window carries whatever its metadata parameter supplied: the author's to configure.
